tasks.title: Find the real bug, reject the plausible-but-false one

findings: running_max([]) raises IndexError because it reads nums[0] without handling an empty input.
median([]) raises IndexError because it tries to average ordered[-1] and ordered[0] when the list is empty.
median([]) raises IndexError because the even-length path reads ordered[-1] and ordered[0] when the list is empty.
running_max((x for x in [1,2])) raises TypeError because it assumes nums is indexable and sliceable.
running_max([]) raises IndexError because it reads nums[0] without checking for an empty input.
median([]) raises IndexError because it indexes ordered[-1] and ordered[0] when nums is empty.

tasks.verdict: no quality gain (18.2x cost) — structure did not help here
